description: Figure 3 presents EBSD-based colored IPF, phase contrast, and KAM maps for three Fe-Mn-Al-C-(Ni) low-density steels—28Mn0Ni [(a)-(c)], 28Mn5Ni [(d)-(f)], and 15Mn5Ni [(g)-(i)]—after hot rolling, 50% cold rolling, and annealing at 1023 K. Subfigures (a, d, g) show austenite IPF maps with respect to the rolling direction (RD) and normal direction (ND), (b, e, h) display phase distributions with FCC in red and B2 in blue, and (c, f, i) give KAM maps scaled from 0 to 5. The Ni-free 28Mn0Ni alloy exhibits nearly fully recrystallized equiaxed austenite grains with low KAM values and only sparse B2 particles. The 28Mn5Ni alloy shows a partially recrystallized microstructure with elongated deformed grains, scattered B2, and heterogeneous KAM; the inset of (d) caption states fine recrystallized austenite grains at higher magnification. The 15Mn5Ni alloy displays a banded two-phase microstructure containing a substantial volume fraction of B2 stringers and high KAM values indicating retained deformation. These maps collectively demonstrate that increasing Ni and decreasing Mn content suppress recrystallization and promote B2 intermetallic formation during annealing at 1023 K.
